meaning: adj.窒息的,憋闷的 透不过气来的
examples: His voice was choking with rage.
Dense smoke swirled and billowed, its rank fumes choking her.
Suddenly she gave a low, choking moan and began to tremble violently.
"The way to understand it is we are rediscovering choking with money, " Ariely says.
Rotella spent years honing his ideas about what keeps people from choking in pressure situations.
